name: Participant Engagement
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant satisfaction → Systemic: Higher retention rates and lower dropout rates → Strategic: Enhanced data quality and increased study power.
options: Incentivize participants with gift cards for each completed sleep cycle
Offer personalized sleep coaching sessions as a value-added service
Provide comprehensive, reimbursed cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT) for anxiety and depression
review: Controls participant burden vs. data quality. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant fatigue.

name: Sensor Technology
consequences: Immediate: Reduced sensor noise → Systemic: Improved event detection and reduced false positives → Strategic: Enhanced data accuracy and increased study reliability.
options: Implement machine learning-based artifact rejection to minimize noise
Utilize advanced signal processing techniques for improved event detection
Integrate novel, non-invasive brain-computer interface (BCI) technology
review: Controls sensor sensitivity vs. participant comfort. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant sleep quality.

name: Data Analysis
consequences: Immediate: Improved event-triage efficiency → Systemic: Enhanced data visualization and reduced manual review burden → Strategic: Increased study productivity and accelerated publication timelines.
options: Develop a cloud-based, collaborative data analysis platform
Implement automated, rule-based event-triage algorithms
Utilize natural language processing (NLP) for enhanced data annotation
review: Controls data sharing vs. participant privacy. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant trust.

name: Staffing and Training
consequences: Immediate: Improved staff productivity → Systemic: Enhanced training programs and reduced turnover rates → Strategic: Increased study efficiency and accelerated publication timelines.
options: Develop a comprehensive, online training program for research technicians
Implement a mentoring program for junior staff members
Utilize telemedicine platforms for remote consultations
review: Controls staffing ratios vs. participant safety. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on staff burnout.

review: Controls recruitment channels vs. participant diversity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant demographics.

options: Implement advanced, non-invasive sleep stage detection technology

review: Controls facility renovations vs. participant safety. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant fatigue.

review: Controls funding sources vs. research priorities. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on research focus.

name: Participant Incentives
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant recruitment → Systemic: Higher per-night compensation → Strategic: Potential for increased participant burnout and decreased retention
options: Standard compensation (€80 per night)
Enhanced compensation (€120 per night) with bonuses for milestone events
Flexible compensation plans tied to participant engagement metrics
review: Controls Participant Burnout vs. Increased Recruitment Efforts. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant fatigue and motivation.

name: Staff Augmentation
consequences: Immediate: Increased staffing capacity → Systemic: More efficient annotation process → Strategic: Potential for decreased inter-rater reliability due to increased staff turnover
options: Hire additional research technicians
Implement a rotational training program for existing staff
Outsource annotation tasks to external vendors
review: Controls Staff Turnover vs. Efficient Annotation Process. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on team cohesion and morale.

name: Data Sharing
consequences: Immediate: Increased data sharing → Systemic: Enhanced collaboration with external researchers → Strategic: Potential for decreased control over data usage and increased risk of intellectual property theft
options: Share de-identified physiological data under data use agreements
Publish raw sensor data in a public repository
Establish a data-sharing platform for collaborative research
review: Controls Data Usage vs. Enhanced Collaboration. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant privacy and confidentiality.

review: Controls Academic Freedom vs. Increased Funding Opportunities. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on research priorities and goals.
